UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the TFOE-PE application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

idPhotoUrl.description: URL to the user's ID photo.

Role.title: Role
Role.description: Represents a user role (e.g., Secretary, Member, Admin).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Role entity.

name.description: Name of the role (e.g., Secretary, Member, Admin).

description.description: Description of the role and its permissions.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document uploaded to the system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

uploadDate.description: Date and time when the document was uploaded.

uploadedByUserId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Document). The user who uploaded the document.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a document category (e.g., Forms, Resolutions, Financials).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.
Unique identifier for the Event entity.

startDate.description: Start date and time of the event.

endDate.description: End date and time of the event.

meetingId.description: The meeting ID for virtual events (e.g., Zoom).

Attendance.title: Attendance
Attendance.description: Represents the attendance of a user at an event.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attendance entity.

attended.description: Indicates whether the user attended the event.

Receipt.title: Receipt
Receipt.description: Represents a receipt generated for a payment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Receipt entity.

userId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Receipt). The user who made the payment.

paymentDate.description: Date and time of the payment.

paymentMethod.description: Payment method used (e.g., GCash, bank transfer, cash).

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification sent to a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the notification was created

AutomatedSummary.title: AutomatedSummary
AutomatedSummary.description: Represents a summary generated by the AI-powered summarization tool.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AutomatedSummary entity.

documentId.description: Reference to Document. (Relationship: Document 1:N AutomatedSummary). The document that was summarized.

parameters.description: Parameters used for summarization (e.g., length, focus areas).

generatedDate.description: Date and time when the summary was generated.

Task.title: Task
Task.description: Represents a to-do item for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Task entity.

title.description: The main description of the task.

completed.description: Indicates whether the task has been completed.

createdAt.description: The date and time when the task was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Document ID is equal to the user's Firebase Auth UID, for easy association. Includes denormalized roleId.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The Firebase Auth UID of the user.

definition.description: Stores individual to-do items for a user. Stored as a subcollection for secure querying.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The UID of the user who owns the task.

definition.description: Stores notifications for a specific user as a subcollection.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The UID of the user who owns the notification.

definition.description: Stores document metadata. Includes denormalized 'uploadedByUserId' for authorization independence.

params.name: attendanceId
params.description: Unique identifier for the attendance record.

definition.description: Stores summaries generated by the AI-powered summarization tool.

params.name: automatedSummaryId
params.description: Unique identifier for the automated summary.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to adhere to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. It leverages denormalization and structural segregation to optimize security rule implementation and data access patterns.  

*   **User Profiles:** User profiles are stored in a collection named `userProfiles` with the document ID matching the user's Firebase Auth UID. This enables path-based ownership for user-specific data, simplifying security rules.
*   **Tasks & Notifications:** Tasks and Notifications are stored in subcollections under each `userProfiles` document. This allows for secure, scalable querying of a user's own data without exposing it to others, following best practices for user-private data.
*   **Roles:** Roles are stored in a `roles` collection, enabling easy management of roles within the application. User profiles store a `roleId` to reference the user's role.
*   **Documents:** Documents are stored in a `documents` collection. The `uploadedByUserId` field in each document denormalizes ownership, enabling secure listing and filtering of documents based on the uploader.
*   **Categories:** Categories are stored in a `categories` collection, providing a simple lookup for document categorization.
*   **Events:** Events are stored in an `events` collection, allowing for easy retrieval and display of event information.
*   **Attendance:** Attendance records are stored in an `attendance` collection. Each document records the user (`userId`) and event (`eventId`).
*   **Receipts:** Receipts are stored in a `receipts` collection and are associated with a `userId` to identify the user who made the payment.
*   **Automated Summaries:** Automated Summaries are stored in the `automatedSummaries` collection, with a `documentId` pointing to the source document.

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**
The structure avoids `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing authorization context. The `documents` collection contains the `uploadedByUserId` field, eliminating the need to fetch the user profile to determine ownership. Other entities like `attendance` and `receipts` are stored in root collections with associated `userId` or `eventId` fields, allowing direct rule application without requiring parent document reads.  This approach ensures that operations remain atomic and security rules are straightforward.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure enables secure `list` operations by segregating data based on ownership and purpose. Private user data (e.g., notifications, receipts) are now stored in user-specific subcollections, ensuring that only the owner can query them. Documents are secured by the `uploadedByUserId` field, allowing filtering of documents that the user has uploaded. The structure supports different security postures by utilizing different collections for data with varying security needs (e.g., public events vs. private user receipts).

By adhering to these principles and strategies, the Firestore structure provides a secure, scalable, and debuggable solution for the application's data storage needs.
